zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey Webb於1372年4月12日出生在英國瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃時，他的父親Henry Alexander Thomas de Richmond Webb21歲，母親Alice Julienne Cloward22歲。他於1403年與Julia Mary de Burton在英國貝德福爾史達科特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1406年在英國瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃被列為reister of the gild of the holy cross of the blessed mary and st. john the baptist of stratford-on-avon (1406-1535)upon-avon.。他去世於1425年在他的家鄉享年53歲。

nameMeanings.name: Webb
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a weaver, from early Middle English webbe (Old English webba (masculine) or webbe (feminine), probably used of both male and female weavers). This word survived into Middle English long enough to give rise to the surname, but was already obsolescent as an agent noun; hence the secondary forms with the agent suffixes -er and -ster (see Webster , Webber and compare Weaver ).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, cognates of 1, including Weber and Weberman.
History: Richard Webb, a Lowland Scot, was an admitted freeman of Boston in 1632, and in 1635 was one of the first settlers of Hartford, CT.

variants.name: Webster
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webbester ‘weaver’ (Old English webbestre ‘female weaver’). By the time of surname formation, the gender distinction of the -stre suffix had almost completely disappeared. Compare Webb , Webber , and Weaver .

History: The name Webster was brought to North America from England independently by several different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One John Webster settled in Ipswich, MA, in 1635; another John Webster (died 1661), ancestor of the lexicographer Noah Webster, emigrated to Cambridge, MA, c. 1631 and later became one of the founders of the colony of CT, of which he was appointed governor in 1656. Daniel Webster (1782–1852), politician and orator, was born in Salisbury, NH, a descendant of Thomas Webster, a prominent 17th-century citizen of Ipswich, MA, whose family had settled there around 1635, while he was still a child.

variants.name: Webber
meanings: English (Devon and Somerset): occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webber, an agent derivative of Webb .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Weber   ‘weaver’.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey Webb was born on 12 April 1372, in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England, his father, Henry Alexander Thomas de Richmond Webb, was 21 and his mother, Alice Julienne Cloward, was 22. He married Julia Mary de Burton in 1403, in Stratford, Bedfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. In 1406, his occupation is listed as reister of the gild of the holy cross of the blessed mary and st. john the baptist of stratford-on-avon (1406-1535)upon-avon. in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England. He died in 1425, in his hometown, at the age of 53.

Il a épousé Julia Mary de Burton en 1403, à Stratford, Bedfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. En 1406, sa profession de son indiquée est reister of the gild of the holy cross of the blessed mary and st. john the baptist of stratford-on-avon (1406-1535)upon-avon. à Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Angleterre. Il est décédé en 1425, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 53 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey Webb(이)가 1372년 4월 12일 영국 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Alexander Thomas de Richmond Webb(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Julienne Cloward(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1403년 영국 베드퍼드실르 스트라트퍼드에서 Julia Mary de Burton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1406년, 그의 직업은 영국 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에서 reister of the gild of the holy cross of the blessed mary and st. john the baptist of stratford-on-avon (1406-1535)upon-avon.이었다. 그는 1425년 그의 고향에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey Webb родился 12 апреля 1372 г. в местности стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry Alexander Thomas de Richmond Webb было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Alice Julienne Cloward, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Julia Mary de Burton в 1403 г. в местности стратфорд, бедфордшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. В 1406 году он работал по профессии reister of the gild of the holy cross of the blessed mary and st. john the baptist of stratford-on-avon (1406-1535)upon-avon. в местности стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия. Он умер в 1425 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 53 лет.
